Trademark registration in India is essential to protect your business name, logo, or symbol. It grants you the exclusive legal right to prevent others from using or imitating your brand identity. The process begins with a detailed trademark search to ensure the availability of your name or logo. At BookMyTM, we specialize in assisting you through every step of the trademark registration process across Kerala, Haryana, New Delhi, Rajasthan, and Uttar Pradesh.

Our experienced team helps you file your trademark application with IP India, a division of the Government of India. We handle all the required documentation and guide you in selecting the appropriate trademark class from the 45 available categories. Once the application is filed, we meticulously track the trademark status until you receive your trademark certificate. Your registered trademark will enjoy protection for ten years, with an easy renewal option to maintain its validity.

If you require global brand protection, BookMyTM facilitates international trademark registration under the Madrid Protocol, simplifying the process of securing your trademark in multiple countries. We also manage the Power of Attorney required for trademark filing and provide additional services such as tax filing. Furthermore, you can display the ™ symbol to indicate your brand is protected.

items.a: A trademark is a unique symbol, word, logo, or design used to represent a business, product, or service. It helps customers distinguish your brand from competitors and legally protects your brand identity from unauthorized use.

Registration grants you the exclusive legal right to use your brand name and logo. It prevents others from copying or stealing your brand identity, builds customer trust, and creates a valuable intangible asset for your business that can be licensed or sold.

items.q: 3. What is the difference between TM, SM, and R?
items.a: TM (Trademark): Used for unregistered goods/products to claim ownership of a brand name or logo.SM (Service Mark): Used for unregistered services rather than physical products.® (Registered): The official symbol used only after your trademark has been fully registered and approved by the Trademark Registry.

items.q: 4. What can be trademarked in India?
items.a: You can trademark business names, product names, logos, symbols, slogans, taglines, packaging designs, sounds, and even unique color combinations that uniquely identify your goods or services.

Trademarks are categorized into 45 different classes based on the nature of the goods and services. Classes 1 to 34 cover physical goods, while Classes 35 to 45 cover services. You must apply for your trademark under the specific class(es) relevant to your business operations.

The process typically involves:Conducting a comprehensive trademark search.Filing the application with the Trademark Registry.Examination by the registry to check for any discrepancies.Publication of the mark in the Trademark Journal.Issuance of the Registration Certificate (if no oppositions are raised).

If the application faces no objections or oppositions, the entire registration process generally takes between 6 to 12 months. However, you can start using the "TM" symbol as soon as your application is filed.

An objection occurs when the Trademark Examiner raises a query regarding your application, often due to similarities with existing marks or a lack of distinctiveness. A formal, legal reply must be filed within 30 days to overcome the objection.

After your mark is published in the Trademark Journal, third parties have a 4-month window to oppose the registration if they believe it infringes on their existing rights. If opposed, both parties must present their evidence to the registry.
A registered trademark in India is valid for 10 years from the date the application was initially filed.

Trademarks can be renewed indefinitely for successive 10-year periods. The renewal application (Form TM-R) can be filed up to 6 months before the trademark's expiration date to ensure continuous protection.
Yes, any individual, sole proprietorship, partnership firm, LLP, private limited company, or NGO can apply for trademark registration.

Trademark infringement is the unauthorized use of a registered trademark (or a confusingly similar mark) by a third party. The registered owner can take legal action to stop the unauthorized use and claim damages.

No, trademark protection is territorial and only valid within India. However, you can use your Indian application as a base to apply for international protection in multiple countries under the Madrid Protocol.

ISO certification is a seal of approval from a third-party body confirming that a company complies with globally recognized ISO management standards. It acts as proof that your business operates with a high level of quality, safety, and efficiency.

Getting ISO certified enhances your brand's credibility, improves operational efficiency, meets global quality benchmarks, makes you eligible for larger tenders, and significantly boosts customer trust.

No, ISO certification is largely voluntary. However, it may be a mandatory requirement or a major advantage when applying for government tenders, signing corporate contracts, or dealing with international clients.

The standard process involves:Identifying the correct ISO standard for your business.Implementing the necessary quality management systems.Conducting an internal audit.Selection of an accredited external certification body.A formal external audit.Issuance of the ISO certificate.
An ISO certification is generally valid for 3 years. To maintain this validity, the organization must undergo mandatory annual surveillance audits to ensure ongoing compliance with the standards.

Certification is the process where an independent body assesses and confirms that a business meets ISO standards.Accreditation is the formal recognition granted to the certification body itself, proving they are authorized and competent to issue certifications.

It is the world’s most recognized standard for Quality Management Systems (QMS). It focuses on consistently meeting customer expectations, maintaining service quality, and driving continuous improvement in business operations.

This is the international standard for Information Security Management Systems (ISMS). It provides a secure framework to help organizations protect sensitive data, such as financial details, intellectual property, and client information, against cyber threats.

Copyright is a legal right that protects original creative works—such as literature, music, art, films, architecture, and software code—preventing others from copying, distributing, or reproducing them without the creator's permission.
Trademark protects brand identifiers like names, logos, and slogans used to market goods or services.Copyright protects original creative and intellectual works like books, music, and software.

A patent is an exclusive legal right granted for a new invention, product, or process. It gives the inventor the right to exclude others from making, using, or selling the invention for a specific period (usually 20 years).
Patent protects the functional aspect, mechanics, or process of a new invention.Trademark protects the branding, name, and logo used to sell that invention to the public.

MSME (Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises) or Udyam Registration is a government certification for businesses in India. It provides access to priority sector lending, lower bank interest rates, government subsidies, and a 50% discount on trademark government filing fees.

Startups recognized by the DPIIT enjoy numerous benefits, including an 80% rebate on patent filing fees, a 50% rebate on trademark filing fees, self-certification under labor laws, income tax exemptions for 3 consecutive years, and access to government funding networks.

The IEC (Import Export Code) is a 10-digit business identification number issued by the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT). It is a mandatory requirement for any business or individual intending to import or export goods and services from India.

A GI (Geographical Indication) tag is a sign used on products that have a specific geographical origin and possess qualities or a reputation that are due to that origin (e.g., Darjeeling Tea, Kanchipuram Silk). It prevents unauthorized parties from using the geographical name.
